source.description: Six-panel generated raster process sheet cropped into five step frames and one finished marker doodle.
source.notes: Created for the 2026-06-03 honest Doodlea.day archive backfill.

process_strategy: One diagonal toothbrush builds from handle and head guides to bristles, grip bumps, rinse droplets, marker fills, and final outline reinforcement without adding new final-only parts.

final_elements.name: diagonal handle
final_elements.description: A long rounded toothbrush handle angled across the page.

final_elements.name: brush head
final_elements.description: A small rounded rectangular head at one end.

final_elements.name: neck curve
final_elements.description: A slight bend between handle and head.

final_elements.name: bristle blocks
final_elements.description: Grouped rounded bristles sitting on the brush head.

final_elements.name: grip bumps
final_elements.description: Small rounded grip marks along the handle.

final_elements.name: rinse droplets
final_elements.description: A few small water drops near the bristles.

final_elements.name: aqua and coral marker fills
final_elements.description: Bright marker color on the already drawn handle, head, and bristles.

final_elements.name: small gray shadow
final_elements.description: A simple shadow beneath the diagonal toothbrush.

frames.visible_job: Places the diagonal handle and brush head as simple rounded shapes.
Adds the neck curve while keeping the handle and head aligned.
Adds grouped bristle blocks onto the existing brush head.
Adds handle grip bumps and a few rinse droplets near the bristles.
Adds aqua and coral marker fills plus a small gray shadow to the existing drawing.

final_step.visible_job: Only thickens existing outlines, evens marker fills, clarifies bristles and droplets, and reinforces the shadow.

rejection_checks: No face, toothpaste, extra brush head, grip mark, bristle group, droplet, color area, or shadow should first appear in the final image.
The toothbrush must stay at the same diagonal angle and scale in every frame.
The final must be bold handmade marker art, not a clean vector icon.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
